evidence: Build/check/test zero-fail on the branch and re-verified on merged main. The adversarial audit ran cross-surface probes against built dist: the classification test genuinely deepStrictEquals currentState's head against validateContext's filtered issues for the same on-disk fixture; custom message templates, error severity, and non-rollup-block invariant declarations all classify identically on both surfaces; stored_status attaches in both divergence directions and never on converged reads (key-census pinned); no second validate pass (diff-verified). Its LOW ordering finding -- items-outer vs invariants-outer emission divergence under multi-invariant configs -- was fixed to validateContext's exact nesting, with the order-sensitive multi-invariant test proven red against the pre-fix source; the fix's fresh re-audit passed with zero findings and independently confirmed ORDERED-EQUAL on the original probe shape. The audit's MEDIUM finding (generated SKILL lag) closed in the post-merge regen commit, deterministically re-checked byte-clean.
